meaning: vt.褰掔撼,姒傛嫭,鎺ㄥ箍,鏅強
examples: It would be foolish to generalize from a single example.
Critics love to generalize, to formulate trends into which all new work must be fitted, however contradictory.
We shouldn't be too quick to generalize that to national characteristics and stereotypes.
He thought to generalize this observation from local and state governments to state and national governments.
Individual clients are still, by and large--it's difficult to generalize but, by and large, still fearful.
